metadata.description: 纯函数: Pure functions always return the same output for the same input and have no side effects. Benefits: they can be cached, tested, and executed in parallel. Python code example to illustrate a pure function: ```python def add(a, b): return a + b # Example usage: result1 = add(2, 3) # result1 will always be 5 result2 = add(2, 3) # result2 will also be 5 print(result1 == result2) # This will print True ``` In this example, the `add` function is a pure function because it takes two inputs and returns the same output every time without any side effects.

data_pointers.title: 纯函数
